title: Relative Clauses
description: Add information to sentences using who, which, that, where, and whose to create natural, complex sentences.

items.back: refers to a person or people
items.example: The man who called me was very polite.

items.back: refers to a thing or animal
items.example: The book which she recommended was excellent.
The film that won the award was incredible.
The city where I grew up has changed a lot.
The student whose essay won the prize is in my class.

items.back: essential information — removes it and meaning changes
items.example: The woman who lives next door is a doctor. (defines which woman)

items.back: extra, non-essential information — can remove it
items.example: My sister, who lives in Madrid, is visiting next week.

items.back: When the relative pronoun is the object, it can be dropped
items.example: The film (that) I watched last night was brilliant.

items.prompt: The professor ___ teaches this course has published three books.
items.explanation: 'Who' is used for people: 'the professor who teaches…'

items.prompt: The hotel ___ we stayed had an amazing rooftop pool.
items.explanation: 'Where' refers to places: 'the hotel where we stayed.' Alternative: the hotel that/which we stayed in.

items.prompt: She has a colleague ___ sister is a famous actress.
items.explanation: 'Whose' shows possession: 'a colleague whose sister…' = the colleague's sister.

items.prompt: The laptop ___ I ordered online arrived damaged.
items.explanation: 'That' or 'which' for things in defining clauses. 'That I ordered' defines which laptop.

items.prompt: My brother, ___ works as an engineer, lives in Singapore.
items.explanation: Non-defining clause with commas → only 'who' (not 'that') for people in non-defining clauses.

activities.title: Complete with the correct relative pronoun

items.sentence: The woman ___ helped me find my lost bag was very kind.
The city ___ I was born has grown enormously in the last decade.
He has a friend ___ father is the mayor of the town.
The documentary ___ we watched last night was incredibly moving.
Her cat, ___ is 15 years old, is still very active and playful.
